title: Graph-Calibrated Battery Thermal Early Warning
summary: A sequential graph-CUSUM study for warning of local battery-pack thermal faults under temperature-sensor drift and dropout.

publish_assets.alt: Two IEEE-oriented line-and-interval charts comparing timely thermal-fault detection and median warning lead time across nominal, sensor-drift, sensor-dropout, and combined conditions.
publish_assets.caption: Four detectors are evaluated on matched synthetic trajectories; proportion intervals are Wilson 95% intervals and lead-time intervals are bootstrap 95% intervals.

publish_assets.alt: A labelled 4 by 4 battery thermal network beside a representative fault trajectory with fault onset, graph-CUSUM alarm, and 60 degree simulation boundary.
publish_assets.caption: The benchmark represents cells as a coupled thermal graph and evaluates whether an alarm arrives before the study boundary.

publish_assets.alt: Five-stage academic methodology diagram from coupled thermal simulation through residual construction, graph evidence pooling, sequential testing, and drift-dropout stress evaluation.
publish_assets.caption: Only the graph-evidence and calibrated sequential-test stages are proposed; calibration and evaluation use disjoint seed ranges.

publish_assets.alt: An interval plot of false-alarm rate against positive sensor drift and a hatched ablation chart under combined drift and sensor dropout.
publish_assets.caption: The robustness sweep and ablation expose the method's detection-versus-false-alarm trade-off instead of reporting accuracy alone.

data_type: Synthetic 4 x 4 coupled thermal network with generated drift, dropout, and local-fault trajectories
research_question: Can common-mode removal and graph-neighbour pooling improve warning before a synthetic battery-pack temperature boundary while keeping false alarms near a held-out calibration target?
limitations: The thermal network is a transparent numerical proxy rather than a chemistry-specific electrochemical or abuse-test model.
The 60 degree Celsius endpoint is a study boundary, not a universal thermal-runaway or safety threshold.
The reported intervals overlap for several comparisons and no instrumented module or deployed battery-management system is evaluated.
documentation_excerpt: This controlled benchmark tests whether graph-aware sequential evidence can preserve warning performance when one battery temperature channel drifts or becomes unavailable.

title: Resilient Water-Network Leak Localisation
summary: A dropout-aware pressure-sensor placement study for localising leaks under measurement noise and single-sensor failure.

publish_assets.alt: Two IEEE-style line charts comparing leak-localisation accuracy across pressure noise and sensor dropout for four placement methods.
publish_assets.caption: The proposed dropout-aware placement is compared with random, degree-centrality, and nominal greedy layouts; error bars show Wilson 95% intervals.

publish_assets.alt: Five-stage IEEE-style methodology diagram from synthetic network construction through leak signatures, dropout-aware sensor placement, localisation, and stress evaluation.
publish_assets.caption: The reproducible evidence pipeline separates the proposed placement step from signature generation, localisation, and Monte Carlo evaluation.

publish_assets.alt: Side-by-side water-network diagrams comparing nominal greedy and dropout-aware pressure sensor locations.
publish_assets.caption: Nominal and dropout-aware sensor layouts on the 36-node synthetic network. The proposed objective spreads measurements across diagnostically distinct regions after sensor loss.

publish_assets.alt: Bar charts of dropout-aware signature separation and top-one and top-three leak-localisation accuracy under the designated stress test.
publish_assets.caption: Ablation and stress test at 8% relative pressure noise and 40% probability of one sensor dropout.

data_type: Synthetic hydraulic-inspired network and generated Monte Carlo benchmark
research_question: Can pressure sensors selected for lower-tail leak-signature separation retain localisation performance when measurements are noisy and one sensor may fail?
limitations: The graph-Laplacian response is a controlled proxy rather than an EPANET or field-calibrated hydraulic model.
Only one unavailable sensor, independent Gaussian noise, and one leak at a time are represented.
The reported advantage is not uniform across every noise level and does not establish field performance.
documentation_excerpt: This reproducible synthetic study tests whether dropout-aware pressure-sensor placement preserves leak-localisation performance when measurements are noisy and one sensor is unavailable.

title: Accessible Multimodal Routing at Gongbei
summary: A cohort-aware routing model studies how slopes, stairs, transfers, crowding and missing elevators change access through a multimodal border hub.

publish_assets.alt: Multilayer Gongbei transport network connecting pedestrian, bus, rail, and taxi nodes
publish_assets.caption: Multilayer network used in the accessible-routing study.

data_type: Case-study network and configured cohort assumptions
research_question: How do mobility constraints change optimal routes and intervention priorities in a dense multimodal hub?
limitations: The case study covers one 1 km² hub.
Several behavioral and accessibility costs require local calibration.
Policy implications are model-based rather than evidence from a deployed intervention.

title: Interpretable EV Dispatch and Replenishment
summary: A white-box pipeline links physics-informed energy prediction, fairness-aware scheduling and disruption-triggered replanning for an electric fleet.

publish_assets.alt: Profit, energy, and fairness trade-off frontier for the EV dispatch experiment
publish_assets.caption: Non-degenerate scheduling frontier from the reported EV dispatch run.

data_type: Project-local operational records; redistribution status not asserted
research_question: Can an auditable model balance fleet energy, profit, fairness and disruption response?
limitations: The reported energy MAPE is 42.512%, despite a high R².
Scheduling uses a simplified assignment structure rather than an exact full-scale MILP.
Disruptions are simulated with a stylized generator.

title: Phantom Traffic-Jam Stability
summary: Microscopic traffic experiments use IDM-style car following to examine when small disturbances grow and how mixed vehicle behavior changes the response.

publish_assets.alt: Space-time heat map of speed in a synthetic microscopic traffic simulation
publish_assets.caption: Synthetic traffic-wave heat map.

data_type: Synthetic traffic simulation
research_question: Under which modeled conditions do local speed disturbances become persistent stop-and-go waves?
limitations: The scenarios are synthetic.
Driver and controller parameters do not establish performance on a real road network.

title: Climate-Driven Dengue on a Spatial Grid
summary: A reaction–diffusion model combines temperature-dependent mosquito ecology, long-range dispersal, vaccination allocation and numerical checks.

publish_assets.alt: Matrix of simulated dengue incidence across synthetic climate and allocation scenarios
publish_assets.caption: Synthetic incidence comparison generated by the experiment matrix.

data_type: Synthetic spatial experiment matrix
research_question: How do climate forcing and spatial allocation assumptions change simulated dengue incidence?
limitations: The published experiment matrix is synthetic.
Outputs are scenario comparisons, not forecasts for a specific city.
Biological parameters require empirical calibration before applied use.

title: Coastal Defence Under Rising Tides
summary: A teaching case combines sea-level scenarios, extreme-value storm surges and cost optimization over a 50-year planning horizon.

publish_assets.alt: Modeled coastal defence cost and risk optimization frontier
publish_assets.caption: Optimization frontier for the stylized coastal-defence teaching case.

data_type: Stylized scenarios and simulated storm surges
research_question: How does uncertainty in sea level and storm surge change the least-cost timing of coastal defences?
limitations: The city and damage functions are stylized.
Results are for modeling instruction, not infrastructure design.

title: SJMMA 2025 Problem Collection
summary: Technical catalogue entry for SJMMA 2025 Problem Collection, recording 46 Python files. Outputs remain unreviewed until promoted into an editorial project overview.
data_type: 17 tracked structured-data files; licensing and provenance require project review
research_question: What technical material is currently present in the SJMMA 2025 Problem Collection project directory?
limitations: This is an inventory-level catalogue entry, not a reviewed research claim.
Code, notebooks, and generated outputs have not been treated as proof of validation or reproducibility.
documentation_excerpt: 1. External CFD wind-field authority (OpenFOAM or MATLAB CFD) produces wind/turbulence fields on a representative urban district. 2. Python samples those fields along an OSM road graph to build edge costs. 3. Improved NSGA-II generates Pareto-optimal routes balancing travel time, energy, and pollen exposure. 4. Dynamic wind (Task 4) is handled by time-sliced field blending and optional recourse re-optimization. 5. Public advice (Task 5) is generated from the Pareto solutions and sensitivity-style results.
